title: prostanthera collina
intro: Prostanthera collina, also known as the 'Native Rosemary' or 'Bush Rosemary', is a flowering plant native to Australia, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in relieving colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Prostanthera collina include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory problems. Prostanthera collina can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
